ENGNew: To the Department Handling the Housewife Beating Murder
changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "To Those on the Housewife Murder Incident"

TextENGNew: Okinomiya Police Station, First Investigative Division
To the Attention of: Division Chief Takasugi
changeReason: New specifies "Division Chief" (課長) rather than generic "Chief" and adds the formal addressing structure matching the Japanese 殿 convention, clarifying Takasugi's specific rank.
Old adds a prefecture police reference not in the Japanese; new accurately translates the department name.
Old uses blank placeholders for the name; new correctly provides 'Naoyuki Sorimachi' from 反町尚之.

TextENGNew: We hereby notify you that a section of the statement records pertaining to a case under this headquarters' jurisdiction has been found to contain material believed to be related to the above-referenced undercover investigation (Okinomiya Police Station Case No. 168, the Hinamizawa Village housewife bludgeoning murder).
changeReason: Old uses placeholder 'X' for case number and 'slaughter' for 撲殺 (bludgeoning murder); new provides correct details.

TextENGNew: During questioning of a suspect arrested in flagrante delicto for violation of the Narcotics Control Act on July 3rd, statements were made that appeared to allude to the commission of the above-referenced crime. It became clear that these statements contained information that could only be known by the perpetrator.
changeReason: New adds arrest date (July 3rd) and specifies legal charge more accurately per the Japanese.

TextENGNew: Accordingly, this headquarters is prepared to furnish your division with a copy of the relevant statement records.
Should these statements be deemed reliable, it is considered highly probable that the suspect in question is the perpetrator of the above-referenced crime.
changeReason: Old fabricates blank placeholder names not in the Japanese; new correctly says 'the suspect in question.'

TextENGNew: It should be noted that upon receiving these statements, the investigating officer inquired with the Okinomiya Police Station regarding this case;

however, the Okinomiya Police Station officer responsible for handling the Undercover Investigation Designation issued by the Prefectural Police Superintendent-General on July 1st (1982, General Administration, Directive I-1-12) misinterpreted the directive,
changeReason: Old mistranslates the directive reference as "B1-12" when the Japanese イ１－１２ should be "I-1-12"; also old says "prefectural police headquarters" while new correctly translates 県警本部長 as "Prefectural Police Superintendent-General" (a person, not a place).

TextENGNew: and failed to properly apprise the investigating officer of the existence of this case.
As a result, the investigating officer did not recognize the significance of the statements in relation to the above-referenced case,
and consequently neglected to conduct an on-site verification or take other necessary measures, allowing the matter to go unaddressed until now. We offer our sincerest apologies for this oversight.
changeReason: Old says "combing the scene" (thoroughly searching) while the Japanese 現場確認 means "on-site verification" (confirming/checking the scene), a meaningfully different investigative action.

TextENGNew: Additionally, we note that the suspect in question died in the holding cell on July 4th.
changeReason: Old gives wrong date (July 7th vs correct July 4th per ７月４日), and fabricates placeholder names.
